title: proxy: Add `OpenImageOptional`
body:
In some code I'm writing I want to be able to cleanly test if an image exists, as distinguished from other errors like authentication problems, network flakes etc.

As best I can tell, the containers/image abstraction doesn't offer a clean way to do this.

For now, I chose the route of adding the ugly string error matching here for the two cases I care about (docker v2s2 registry and oci directories), so my Rust code can operate in terms of clean `Option<Image>`.

comments.body: @cgwalters can  you update your PR, we are getting ready for a release.
I’d still like to insist on `isNotFoundImageError` using Go types instead of string matches; the past experience with such “short-term things to be cleaned up” in `inspect.go` has been painful.

> I’d still like to insist on isNotFoundImageError using Go types instead of string matches; the past experience with such “short-term things to be cleaned up” in inspect.go has been painful.

OK yep fair; to be clear please do go ahead and do a skopeo release without this PR, this PR is not in any kind of critical path and I may not have time to circle back for a bit.
OK, cleaned this up a bit more, I think comments should be addressed.  But I do feel guilty for not trying to dive in to actually adding proper c/image APIs for this...

BTW while I'm here...it's not related strictly to this PR and I think you know this but:

I want to highlight that we are using this proxy code to update the operating system by default in OCP 4.12 https://lists.fedoraproject.org/archives/list/devel@lists.fedoraproject.org/message/CZ7UNVVXXOFIMOPD6YCCU6D7DSX2NBDJ/
